question.text: An emergency fund should ideally be adequate to cover expenses for:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** An emergency fund should be adequate to meet expenses for approximately six months, in case regular income becomes unavailable. A ratio of 4-6 months is considered comfortable. **The Trap:** 12-18 months is too conservative and locks up too much money in low-yielding liquid assets. 1-2 months is inadequate for most contingencies. **Cross-Exam Context:** Emergency fund sizing is tested in X-A (Ch 3) and XVII (Retirement Adviser). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Build the emergency fund FIRST before starting goal-based investing — this is the foundation of all financial planning.
